items.title: Dynamic Programming
items.description: Greedy commits to one choice; DP keeps all sub-problem answers and combines them. When the greedy-choice property fails, fall back to DP.

items.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
items.description: Many greedy algorithms (Dijkstra, Huffman, Prim) need 'extract the current best' — a textbook priority-queue job.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Most greedy algorithms start with a sort (by deadline, ratio, score) — a one-time O(n log n) cost.

items.title: Graphs
items.description: Dijkstra, Prim and Kruskal are greedy graph algorithms; they all repeatedly take the locally cheapest safe step.

items.title: Problem-Solving Patterns
items.description: Greedy is one of the standard interview-pattern templates alongside sliding-window, two-pointer, DP and backtracking.
